data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260506T170122-3b9a6ef7/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260506T170122-3b9a6ef7/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260506T170122-3b9a6ef7/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260506T170122-3b9a6ef7/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260506T170122-3b9a6ef7/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-d0c656b0">
    <title>cli: add ddx bead queue top move clear</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
The spec now defines first-class queue movement commands, but operators still only have the generic `ddx bead update --set queue-rank=...` escape hatch. That forces humans to calculate ranks manually and does not protect priority semantics.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:121-137 defines `ddx bead queue top`, `ddx bead queue move --before/--after`, and `ddx bead queue clear` semantics.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:254-261 lists the CLI surface.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:327-337 defines user-story acceptance for queue movement.
- cli/cmd/bead.go:46-65 registers bead subcommands but has no queue subcommand.
- cli/cmd/bead.go:448-540 supports generic update/set/unset but no command that computes sparse ranks or rejects cross-priority moves.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add `ddx bead queue` with `top <id>`, `move <id> --before <other-id>`, `move <id> --after <other-id>`, and `clear <id>`.
- Implement sparse integer rank calculation: top assigns before the first ranked/effective bead in the same priority; before/after uses midpoint when possible; if no midpoint exists, renormalize only the affected priority bucket.
- Reject before/after moves across different priorities with a clear error; priority changes remain explicit via `ddx bead update --priority`.
- Persist queue-rank as a JSON number in Bead.Extra["queue-rank"]; clear removes the key.
- Register the subcommand in newBeadCommand.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Ready/ReadyExecution sorting in this bead; it depends on ddx-5ca2b0af.
- Do not introduce a server-side picker or global queue table.
- Do not add queue-rank to the core Bead struct or known schema field map.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. TestBeadQueueTopSetsRankWithoutChangingPriority verifies `ddx bead queue top <id>` persists numeric queue-rank and leaves Priority unchanged.
2. TestBeadQueueMoveBeforeAfterUsesSparseRanks verifies before/after placement produces the expected ReadyExecution order without rewriting unrelated priority buckets.
3. TestBeadQueueMoveRejectsCrossPriority verifies before/after across different priorities returns a clear error and does not mutate either bead.
4. TestBeadQueueClearRemovesQueueRank verifies `ddx bead queue clear <id>` removes the key and default ordering resumes.
5. TestBeadQueueRenormalizesOnlyPriorityBucket verifies midpoint exhaustion renormalizes only beads in the affected priority bucket.
6. WIRED-IN: newBeadCommand registers `newBeadQueueCommand`, and `ddx bead queue --help` lists top, move, and clear.
7. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run "TestBeadQueue" -count=1 passes.
8. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/... passes.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:cli, area:beads, kind:feature, spec:FEAT-004</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-0a47840f" spec-id="FEAT-004" base-rev="a927ecad219e870368ebfe0945027d28f1b9b102" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260506T170122-3b9a6ef7"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-004" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md">Feature: Beads (Work Item Tracker)</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output_summary: out=7.7KB 260 lines "// Package bead implements the on-disk bead t..."
data.output_excerpt: // Package bead implements the on-disk bead t...

data.output:
This bead is already implemented in the current worktree, so I verified it rather than editing code. No files changed and no commit was needed from this pass.

Evidence:
- `newBeadCommand` registers `newBeadQueueCommand` in [cli/cmd/bead.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-d0c656b0-20260506T170122-3b9a6ef7/cli/cmd/bead.go#L46-L58).
- The CLI surface for `ddx bead queue top|move|clear` is implemented in [cli/cmd/bead_queue.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-d0c656b0-20260506T170122-3b9a6ef7/cli/cmd/bead_queue.go#L9-L90).
- The store-side sparse-rank behavior, cross-priority rejection, and clear behavior are implemented in [cli/internal/bead/queue.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-d0c656b0-20260506T170122-3b9a6ef7/cli/internal/bead/queue.go#L8-L220).
- Coverage for the acceptance cases is in [cli/cmd/bead_queue_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-d0c656b0-20260506T170122-3b9a6ef7/cli/cmd/bead_queue_test.go#L46-L203).

Verification:
- `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run 'TestBeadQueue' -count=1`
- `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/...`
- `lefthook run pre-commit`

Both Go test commands and the pre-commit hook passed.
